concept: Run basic input/output, variables, arithmetic, conditions, loops, and functions.
title: Eligibility counter
statement: Given a list of scores and a cutoff, return how many scores are greater than or equal to the cutoff. Do not use built-in filtering helpers; practice loops and conditions.
constraints: Use Python 3 only.
Return the answer; do not print unless explicitly asked.
Handle empty and boundary cases where meaningful.
Write your own logic; do not call external libraries except collections, heapq, or math when appropriate.
hidden_test_ideas: case that breaks the naive approach
hints: Write the brute-force idea in one sentence first.
Ask: what information does Python Diagnostic preserve or eliminate?
Dry run on the sample before coding.
Only request AI help after one failed attempt and one written explanation.
solution_approach: Initialize count to zero, scan each score, and increment only when the condition is true.
common_mistakes: Skipping edge cases
Changing input unexpectedly
Not explaining why the chosen pattern applies
Confusing correctness with passing only the sample
interview_followups: Explain the invariant or decision rule.
What changes if the input is larger?
Can you reduce extra space?
Which test case would you add first?
